title: National policy on flu vaccination for older people
subtitle: This shows which countries have a national policy to recommend flu vaccines to older people.
caption: Across the globe in 2020, national policies regarding flu vaccination for older people show a distinct geographic divide. The map displays countries colored to indicate whether they have a national policy recommending flu vaccines for this demographic. A large majority of nations, including the United States, Canada, Brazil, Australia, and most of Europe and Asia, are shaded blue, indicating that vaccination is recommended. In contrast, a smaller set of countries, primarily located in North Africa, the Middle East, and parts of Southeast Asia such as Indonesia and the Philippines, are shaded red, signifying no national recommendation. Areas with no available data, including Greenland and parts of central Africa, are marked with diagonal hatching. The visual pattern shows that a national recommendation for flu vaccination among older people is the dominant policy stance worldwide, with notable exceptions concentrated in specific regions.
